title: botrychium silaifolium var. coulteri
intro: Botrychium silaifolium var. coulteri, also known as Coulteri's rattlesnake fern or Coulteri's wood fern, is a type of rare and endangered plant species belonging to the Ophioglossaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Botrychium silaifolium var. coulteri include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant may include infusions, teas, and tinctures, although its use is highly regulated due to its endangered status and potential side effects.
